evals.prompt: Run /z-eval-judge on a llm.yml where one case has verbosity 3.8 and another has confidence 0.22.
evals.expected_output: The judge writes a judge: block with at least one finding per anomaly, each with severity warn or flag, and at least one recommendation. The original totals and aggregates are preserved.
assertions: llm.yml has a top-level judge: block appended
At least one finding flags verbosity > 2.0 for the high-verbosity case
At least one finding flags confidence < 0.4 for the low-confidence case
The original totals block is unchanged
The original aggregates block is unchanged

evals.prompt: Run /z-eval-judge on a llm.yml where brittle-case lists several `contains` graders whose `matched_token` needles are only two characters each (loose substring checks).
evals.expected_output: The judge flags those grader rows, ties recommendations to regex or llm-judge style scorers, and surfaces a structured needs_user_input hand-off toward /z-eval-update instead of editing eval sources inline.
assertions: At least one finding has dimension: grader for each flagged case
The detail on each grader finding quotes each matched_token needle (or equivalent quoted substring) and ties it to fewer than four characters of meaningful evidence
Recommendations contrast loose `contains`/`matched_token` substring checks with regex matchers or llm-judge scorers instead of vague "edit eval definitions" wording alone
needs_user_input (or equivalent structured questions/options) routes remediation through /z-eval-update without silent edits to eval registrations
No evals.json files were modified by this skill directly

evals.expected_output: The skill aborts cleanly, pointing the user to /z-eval-execute.
assertions: No files were created or modified
The skill printed a pointer to /z-eval-execute
The skill did not crash

evals.prompt: Cursor just finished wiring the newest LLM eval batch. Pull up the adversarial judge evals guidance, open the freshest `_runs` slice under our configured evals folder, ingest the three YAML aggregates plus the raw case logs, and append a sceptical critique without rerun.
assertions: Before touching artefacts the agent reads `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` and honours both `evalsDir` and `judgeModel` when naming the model inside the new `judge` stanza.
The agent chooses the single newest directory under `{evalsDir}/_runs/`, then loads `static.yml`, `llm.yml`, `report.yml`, and each relevant `logs/<case>.log` entry together.

The agent appends a `judge` block where each `findings` item names a concrete case id, restricts `dimension` to verbosity, accuracy, confidence, grader, or assertion, and keeps `severity` within warn or flag.
The agent lists concrete `recommendations` strings such as grader upgrades or updater follow-ups without deleting or rewriting pre-existing `totals` or `aggregates` keys in `llm.yml`.
The agent refrains from launching eval executors or otherwise re-executing cases during the review pass.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases exercise post-hoc scan of the freshest `_runs` bundle, sceptical grading heuristics, append-only enrichment of `llm.yml`, explicit failure when nothing is runnable yet, and updater handoffs expressed only as YAML `needs_user_input` rather than interactive prompts from this skill.

evals.expected_output: The agent cites the resolved run timestamp, summarizes cross-case risks, updates only the trailing judge section in `llm.yml`, leaves prior totals untouched, and never schedules another evaluation pass.

evals.prompt: I need the coverage critic on our eval harness, but this checkout has never written a timestamped folder under `_runs`. Route me through the correct recovery action.
assertions: The agent states that no eligible timestamped directory exists beneath `{evalsDir}/_runs/` and points the operator toward `/z-eval-execute` before attempting analysis.
The agent does not append a `judge` block or alter `llm.yml` because there is nothing to adjudicate.
The agent avoids inventing sandbox paths outside the declared `evalsDir` hierarchy.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases exercise post-hoc scan of the freshest `_runs` bundle, sceptical grading heuristics, append-only enrichment of `llm.yml`, explicit failure when nothing is runnable yet, and updater handoffs expressed only as YAML `needs_user_input` rather than interactive prompts from this skill.
fixture_justifications: Supplies the judge configuration path the skill names so the agent resolves `evalsDir` predictably inside the sandbox.
Materializes `_runs/` without any timestamped child directory so Step 1 must fail instead of loading imaginary artefacts.

evals.expected_output: The agent explains that no recent run directory exists, tells the operator to execute the suite first, and never fabricates `judge` output.

evals.prompt: Load the adversarial evaluator skill, focus on run `20260201T090000Z`, and tell me whether our graders and soft metrics deserve tightening before release.
assertions: The agent records at least one `findings` row whose `dimension` equals assertion when eval assertions lack matching grader corroboration for the same case.
The agent records a `findings` row whose `dimension` equals grader when a contains grader depends on fewer than four characters of evidence.
The agent records `findings` tied to verbosity above 2.0, confidence under 0.4, and accuracy under 0.5 when those thresholds appear for any case.

When brittle contains graders are flagged the subsequent `recommendations` cite `matched_token` or `kind: contains` risk explicitly and steer operators toward regex matchers or llm-backed judges rather than generic assertion tweaks alone.
When durations are interpretable versus the suite mean, anomalies beyond two sigma are surfaced as severity warn or flag instead of silently passing.
The resulting `llm.yml` retains the preceding `totals` and `aggregates` verbatim while nesting the appended `judge` content afterward.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases exercise post-hoc scan of the freshest `_runs` bundle, sceptical grading heuristics, append-only enrichment of `llm.yml`, explicit failure when nothing is runnable yet, and updater handoffs expressed only as YAML `needs_user_input` rather than interactive prompts from this skill.
fixture_justifications: Provides the configuration file cited by the skill so `evalsDir` resolves to `evals`.
Offers static backend totals the skill merges with llm-derived signals.
Seeds contradictory assertion coverage versus grader transcripts plus risky soft metrics and several sub-four-character contains graders for heuristic review.
Supplies merged rollup YAML the workflow reads alongside per-backend files.
Captures runnable log evidence the skill cross-checks against structured metrics.

files.content:
totals:
  cases: 2
aggregates:
  mean_duration_ms: 1200
  stddev_duration_ms: 95

cases:
  noisy-case:
    verbosity: 2.9
    confidence: 0.35
    accuracy: 0.45
    duration_ms: 5200
  brittle-case:
    graders:
      - kind: contains
        matched_token: "f0"
      - kind: contains
        matched_token: "1x"
      - kind: contains
        matched_token: "z"
    duration_ms: 1185
assertions_checked:
  noisy-case:
    - id: parity
      satisfied: false

prompt: reconcile metrics
response: stretched narrative
grader_contains: PASS
assertion_parity: FAIL

evals.expected_output: The agent writes nuanced findings tying log evidence to verbosity, accuracy, confidence, assertion gaps, and feeble graders, obeying merge-only edits on `llm.yml`.

evals.prompt: The judge surfaced weak assertions on two skills — prepare the guarded hand-off exactly as documented so `/z-eval-judge` can route the prompts, and do not open interactive questions yourself.
assertions: The emitted hand-off includes `needs_user_input.reason` plus a `questions` array whose first item uses `id: handoff`, offers labelled `options`, and aligns with updater delegation wording from the workflow.
The agent never invokes `askQuestion` or analogous interactive widgets from inside the judged skill reasoning path.
The narrative names specific downstream skills or eval targets deserving `/z-eval-update`, matching the sceptical findings it already enumerated.
No `plugins/**/evals/evals.json` files nor other eval registrations are rewritten during this step; escalation stays descriptive.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases exercise post-hoc scan of the freshest `_runs` bundle, sceptical grading heuristics, append-only enrichment of `llm.yml`, explicit failure when nothing is runnable yet, and updater handoffs expressed only as YAML `needs_user_input` rather than interactive prompts from this skill.

evals.expected_output: The agent returns a YAML `needs_user_input` fragment with enumerated options covering batch approval, supervised updates, or cancellation, referencing affected skill targets implicitly or explicitly.

evals.prompt: /z-eval-judge is available now — treat that palette entry exactly like the sceptical evaluator skill: reuse the artefacts from the freshest suite run and summarise what still looks fragile.
assertions: The interaction transcript lists reading `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` prior to traversing `_runs/`, preserving the documented ordering.
Reported outcomes remain post-hoc: no runner scripts are re-triggered unless the operator explicitly exits this flow to execute something else.
When risk warrants follow-up automation, structured YAML matches the prescribed `needs_user_input` scaffold instead of ad hoc prose questionnaires.

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases exercise post-hoc scan of the freshest `_runs` bundle, sceptical grading heuristics, append-only enrichment of `llm.yml`, explicit failure when nothing is runnable yet, and updater handoffs expressed only as YAML `needs_user_input` rather than interactive prompts from this skill.

evals.expected_output: Even when invoked via the palette hook, behaviour matches the textual workflow loads, critiques, merge-only edits, and optional structured hand-off payloads.
